steps.title: Find the slope of line s
steps.content: Rewrite $8x+2y=0$ as $$2y=-8x$$ $$y=-4x.$$ Therefore line $s$ has slope $-4$.

steps.title: Build the perpendicular line
steps.content: A perpendicular line has slope $\frac{1}{4}$, the negative reciprocal of $-4$. Since line $t$ passes through $(6,0)$, write $$y=\frac{1}{4}(x-6).$$

steps.title: Match the point with y = 5/4
steps.content: The point $(k,\frac{5}{4})$ lies on line $t$, so set $y=\frac{5}{4}$: $$\frac{5}{4}=\frac{1}{4}(k-6).$$ Multiplying by $4$ gives $5=k-6$, so $k=11$.

steps.title: Confirm on the graph
steps.content: In Desmos, the line $y=\frac{1}{4}(x-6)$ meets $y=\frac{5}{4}$ at $x=11$. Therefore the value of $k$ is 11.
